member.name: T:Etch.Analyzers.NoInterpolatedLogAnalyzer
member.summary: ET0106 — forbids passing an interpolated string ($"...") as the template argument to any IEtchLogger.Log overload, or to any extension method on IEtchLogger that accepts a string-like handler. Structured logging's zero-allocation guarantee depends on passing a literal template plus a stackalloced KVP span; interpolated strings allocate on every call and defeat that contract.

remarks.para: Scope. Only syntax trees whose file path contains a /src/ segment, excluding /src-gen/, /tests/, /tools/. Applies only to assemblies whose name begins with Etch. and is not a test assembly.

Detection. Roslyn marks an $"..." literal as InterpolatedStringExpressionSyntax. We scan InvocationExpressionSyntax nodes whose called method is named Log and inspect the template positional or named argument for that type.

member.name: T:Etch.Analyzers.NoRawThrowAnalyzer
member.summary: ET0108 — forbids raw throw new <Exception-derived>(...) inside the Etch production assemblies (Etch.* under src/). D-006 mandates that every internal failure path funnels through Panic.Invariant / Panic.ArgumentNull / etc., which in turn construct EtchException with a stable ET-P-#### panic code. Raw throws slip past that contract and produce incident reports with no machine-readable code, so the rule is an error, not a warning.

remarks.para: Scope. Only syntax trees whose file path contains a /src/ (or \src\) segment. Files under /src-gen/, /tests/, and /tools/ are exempt: generators produce wrapper code, tests exercise the real BCL, and tooling lives outside the production contract.

item.description: EtchException itself — the Panic helpers construct it directly.
OperationCanceledException — cooperative cancellation signal; framework convention.
ObjectDisposedException — framework convention for post-dispose use.

member.name: T:Etch.Analyzers.PublicMustHaveDocsAnalyzer
member.summary: ET1501 — every public API in a shipping assembly must have an XML documentation comment.
